tools.name: tool.catalog
tools.description: [compact alias of tool_catalog] Discover available tools including hidden legacy tools. Use query to search by capability keywords (e.g., animation, import, tilemap, audio).

query.description: Optional keyword search over tool names and descriptions.

limit.description: Maximum results to return. Default: 30, max: 100.

tools.name: project.list
tools.description: [compact alias of list_projects] Scans a directory for Godot projects (folders containing project.godot). Use to discover projects before using other tools. Returns array of {path, name}.

directory.description: Absolute path to search (e.g., "/home/user/godot-projects" on Linux, "C:\Games" on Windows)

recursive.description: If true, searches all subdirectories. If false (default), only checks immediate children.

tools.name: project.info
tools.description: [compact alias of get_project_info] Returns metadata about a Godot project including name, version, main scene, autoloads, and directory structure. Use to understand project before modifying. Requires valid project.godot.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

tools.name: project.search
tools.description: [compact alias of search_project] Searches for text or regex patterns across project files. Use to find function usages, variable references, or TODOs. Returns file paths and line numbers.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

regex.description: If true, treats query as regex. Default: false

caseSensitive.description: If true, case-sensitive search. Default: false

tools.name: editor.launch
tools.description: [compact alias of launch_editor] Opens the Godot editor GUI for a project. Use when visual inspection or manual editing of scenes/scripts is needed. Opens a new window on the host system. Requires: project directory with project.godot file.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

tools.name: editor.run
tools.description: [compact alias of run_project] Launches a Godot project in a new window and captures output. Use to test gameplay or verify script behavior. Runs until stop_project is called. Use get_debug_output to retrieve logs.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

scene.description: Optional: specific scene to run (e.g., "scenes/TestLevel.tscn"). If omitted, runs main scene from project settings.

tools.name: editor.stop
tools.description: [compact alias of stop_project] Terminates the currently running Godot project process. Use to stop a project started with run_project. No effect if no project is running.

reason.description: Brief explanation of why you are calling this tool

tools.name: editor.debug_output
tools.description: [compact alias of get_debug_output] Retrieves console output and errors from the currently running Godot project. Use after run_project to check logs, errors, and print statements. Returns empty if no project is running.

reason.description: Brief explanation of why you are calling this tool

tools.name: editor.status
tools.description: [compact alias of get_editor_status] Returns the connection status of the Godot Editor Plugin bridge. Use to check if the editor is connected before using scene/resource tools that require the editor plugin.

tools.name: scene.create
tools.description: [compact alias of create_scene] Creates a new Godot scene file (.tscn) with a specified root node type. Use to start building new game levels, UI screens, or reusable components. The scene is saved automatically after creation.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

scenePath.description: Path for new scene file relative to project (e.g., "scenes/Player.tscn", "levels/Level1.tscn")

rootNodeType.description: Godot node class for root (e.g., "Node2D" for 2D games, "Node3D" for 3D, "Control" for UI). Default: "Node"

tools.name: scene.node.add
tools.description: [compact alias of add_node] Adds ANY node type to an existing scene. This is the universal node creation tool — replaces all specialized create_* node tools. Supports ALL ClassDB node types (Camera3D, DirectionalLight3D, AudioStreamPlayer, HTTPRequest, RayCast3D, etc.). Set any property via the properties parameter with type conversion support (Vector2, Vector3, Color, etc.). Use query_classes to discover available node types. Use query_class_info to discover available properties for a type.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

scenePath.description: Path to .tscn file relative to project (e.g., "scenes/Player.tscn")

parentNodePath.description: Node path within scene (e.g., "." for root, "Player" for direct child, "Player/Sprite2D" for nested)

nodeType.description: Godot node class name (e.g., "Sprite2D", "CollisionShape2D", "CharacterBody2D"). Must be valid Godot 4 class.

nodeName.description: Name for the new node (will be unique identifier in scene tree)

properties.description: Optional properties to set on the node (as JSON string)

tools.name: scene.save
tools.description: [compact alias of save_scene] Saves changes to a scene file or creates a variant at a new path. Most scene modification tools save automatically, but use this for explicit saves or creating variants.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

scenePath.description: Path to .tscn file relative to project (e.g., "scenes/Player.tscn")

newPath.description: Optional: New path to save as variant (e.g., "scenes/PlayerBlue.tscn")

tools.name: script.create
tools.description: [compact alias of create_script] Creates a new GDScript (.gd) file with optional templates. Use to generate scripts for game logic. Templates: "singleton" (autoload), "state_machine" (FSM), "component" (modular), "resource" (custom Resource).

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

scriptPath.description: Path for new script relative to project (e.g., "scripts/player.gd", "autoload/game_manager.gd")

content.description: Optional: initial script content to add after class declaration

reason.description: Optional reason/context for this change. Displayed in visualizer audit timeline.

tools.name: script.modify
tools.description: [compact alias of modify_script] Adds functions, variables, or signals to an existing GDScript. Use to extend scripts without manual editing. Supports @export, @onready annotations and type hints.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

scriptPath.description: Path to existing .gd file relative to project (e.g., "scripts/player.gd")

name.description: Name of the function, variable, or signal

params.description: For functions/signals: parameter string (e.g., "delta: float, input: Vector2")

isExport.description: For variables: whether to add @export annotation

isOnready.description: For variables: whether to add @onready annotation

reason.description: Optional reason/context for this change. Displayed in visualizer audit timeline.

tools.name: script.info
tools.description: [compact alias of get_script_info] Analyzes a GDScript and returns its structure: functions, variables, signals, class_name, extends. Use before modify_script to understand existing code.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

scriptPath.description: Path to .gd file relative to project (e.g., "scripts/player.gd")

includeInherited.description: If true, includes members from parent classes. Default: false (only script-defined members).

tools.name: class.query
tools.description: [compact alias of query_classes] Query available Godot classes from ClassDB with filtering. Use to discover node types, resource types, or any class before using add_node/create_resource. Categories: node, node2d, node3d, control, resource, physics, physics2d, audio, visual, animation.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot.

filter.description: Optional: substring filter for class names (case-insensitive, e.g., "light", "collision")

category.description: Optional: filter by category (node, node2d, node3d, control, resource, physics, physics2d, audio, visual, animation)

instantiableOnly.description: If true, only return classes that can be instantiated (default: false)

tools.name: class.info
tools.description: [compact alias of query_class_info] Get detailed information about a specific Godot class: methods, properties, signals, enums. Use to discover available properties before calling add_node/create_resource/set_node_properties, or to find methods before call_runtime_method.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot.

includeInherited.description: If true, include inherited members from parent classes (default: false — shows only class-specific members)

tools.name: runtime.status
tools.description: [compact alias of get_runtime_status] Checks if a Godot game instance is running and connected for live debugging. Use before other runtime tools.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to project directory containing project.godot. Use the same path across all tool calls in a workflow.

tools.name: visualizer.map
tools.description: [compact alias of map_project] Crawl the entire Godot project and build an interactive visual map of all scripts showing their structure (variables, functions, signals), connections (extends, preloads, signal connections), and descriptions. Opens an interactive browser-based visualization at localhost:6505.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to the Godot project directory

root.description: Root path to start crawling from (default: res://)

include_addons.description: Whether to include scripts in addons/ folder (default: false)

tools.name: lsp.diagnostics
tools.description: [compact alias of lsp_get_diagnostics] Get GDScript diagnostics from Godot Language Server for a script file.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to Godot project root

scriptPath.description: Path to script relative to project root

tools.name: dap.output
tools.description: [compact alias of dap_get_output] Get captured Godot DAP console output lines
